name: Qdrant
what_it_does: High-performance open-source vector database written in Rust with an official MCP server, supporting dense and sparse vectors, payload filtering, and hybrid search for AI applications.
use_cases: RAG pipelines with semantic document retrieval and metadata filtering
Hybrid search combining dense embeddings with sparse BM42 keyword vectors
AI agent semantic memory via the official MCP server
Recommendation systems using vector similarity with business logic filters
Real-time vector search at scale with on-disk indexing for large datasets
not_for: Teams that want full managed infrastructure without any Rust/Docker knowledge
Simple keyword search without semantic understanding
best_when: You want a high-performance, resource-efficient open-source vector database with first-class MCP server support and the flexibility to self-host or use Qdrant Cloud.
avoid_when: You need a purely managed SaaS experience with no operational overhead, or your workload is purely keyword-based.

auth.notes: API key auth via X-API-Key header or Bearer token. Read-only keys and full-access keys supported. No auth required for local/open instances. Qdrant Cloud uses project-scoped API keys.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free cluster on Qdrant Cloud: 1GB RAM, 0.5 vCPU — self-hosted is unlimited
pricing.paid_tiers: Qdrant Cloud from ~$0.014/hour per vCPU; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Apache-2.0 open source. Self-hosting is completely free. Qdrant Cloud is managed; free tier is permanent (not trial). Very cost-efficient compared to competing managed services.

performance.rate_limits: No hard rate limits on self-hosted; cloud limits scale with plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST errors include status, error type, and detailed description. gRPC errors follow standard status codes. Documentation is comprehensive with many examples.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Points (vectors) are identified by ID (UUID or unsigned int). Upsert is idempotent — inserting the same ID replaces rather than duplicates. Batch upserts are safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: Collection must be created with the correct vector size before insertion — size mismatches cause hard errors
gRPC is significantly faster than REST for bulk operations — use gRPC in latency-sensitive agents
Payload indexes must be explicitly created for filtered search performance — missing indexes cause full scans
Sparse vector search (BM42) requires explicit sparse vector configuration at collection creation
MCP server requires QDRANT_URL env var — QDRANT_LOCAL_PATH and QDRANT_URL cannot both be set
MCP server uses FastEmbed for embedding — first startup downloads the model (~40MB)
